name: Pace Resources
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving 700+ members with checking, savings, loans, and credit card services. Established 1969 with ~$7M in assets.
description_long:
PACE Resources Federal Credit Union was originally chartered as Buchart-Horn Federal Credit Union on June 9, 1969, to serve employees and families of Buchart-Horn, Inc. The organization was renamed in 1973 to serve employees of PACE Resources Inc. and its subsidiaries. In 1994, Topflight Federal Credit Union merged with PACE Resources, consolidating membership and services. Today, the credit union operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned institution with over 700 members and approximately $7 million in assets.

PACE Resources offers a comprehensive range of financial services comparable to traditional banks, including checking and savings accounts, club savings accounts, auto loans, bill consolidation loans, home equity lines of credit, signature loans, and a VISA credit card. The credit union provides online banking, loan applications, rate information, and debit card services. They also offer specialized products like Vacation Club accounts and VISA Gift Cards. Loan calculators are available on their website to help members estimate monthly, bi-weekly, or weekly payments.

The credit union operates under the cooperative philosophy of "People helping people" and maintains a policy of "once a member, always a member," allowing members to retain membership regardless of employment status changes. Membership requires opening and maintaining a savings account with a minimum $5 opening deposit—significantly lower than many traditional banks. The organization emphasizes efficient, effective management and progressive asset strategy to serve current and future members through management initiatives and membership input.

As a small credit union, PACE Resources has limited physical locations (York Office and Adhesives Research Office with restricted hours) and modest asset size compared to regional or national banks. Members should verify that the limited office hours and small membership base align with their banking needs. The credit union's focus on member service and not-for-profit structure typically offers competitive rates, but prospective members should confirm specific rate offerings before joining.
pros: Not-for-profit member-owned structure prioritizes member benefits over shareholder profits
Extremely low minimum deposit requirement of $5 to open and maintain savings account
"Once a member, always a member" policy allows membership continuation regardless of employment status
Competitive product range including auto loans, bill consolidation, HELOC, signature loans, and credit cards
Online banking and loan application capabilities for remote member access
Loan calculator tool available to estimate payments across different payment schedules
Direct member communication through recent posts and newsletter updates on rates and specials
cons: Very limited physical locations with restricted hours (York office and one other location)
Small membership base (~700 members) may indicate limited resources or service infrastructure
Modest asset size ($7 million) compared to regional or national credit unions and banks
Adhesives Research Office restricts visitors and requires advance phone calls
York Office has limited Friday hours (8am-2pm only) and closure for lunch 12:30pm-1:30pm Monday-Thursday
best_for: Current and former employees of PACE Resources Inc. and its subsidiaries seeking credit union membership
Members prioritizing not-for-profit, member-owned banking philosophy over traditional bank structures
Borrowers seeking competitive rates on auto loans, bill consolidation, or home equity credit lines
Consumers who value relationship banking and local credit union support
diagnosis: PACE Resources FCU is best suited for current and former PACE Resources Inc. employees and their families seeking member-owned, not-for-profit banking with competitive loan products. The primary caveat is the very limited physical locations and restricted hours, making this credit union impractical for members requiring frequent in-person service or flexible office access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
